42:     def get_status_line(self) -> str:
43:         parts = ["[1] Public"]
44:         
45:         for i, channel in enumerate(self.active_channels):
46:             parts.append(f"[{i + 2}] {channel}")
47:         
48:         dm_start = 2 + len(self.active_channels)
49:         for i, (nick, _) in enumerate(self.active_dms.items()):
50:             parts.append(f"[{i + dm_start}] DM:{nick}")
51:         
52:         return f"Active: {' '.join(parts)}"
53:     
54:     def switch_to_number(self, num: int) -> bool:
55:         if num == 1:
56:             self.current_mode = Public()
57:             print("\033[90m─────────────────────────\033[0m")
58:             print("\033[90m» Switched to Public chat. Just type to send messages.\033[0m")
59:             return True
60:         
61:         channel_end = 1 + len(self.active_channels)
62:         if 1 < num <= channel_end:
63:             channel_idx = num - 2
64:             if channel_idx < len(self.active_channels):
65:                 channel = self.active_channels[channel_idx]
66:                 self.current_mode = Channel(channel)
67:                 print("\033[90m─────────────────────────\033[0m")
68:                 print(f"\033[90m» Switched to channel {channel}\033[0m")
69:                 return True
70:         
71:         dm_start = channel_end + 1
72:         dm_idx = num - dm_start
73:         dm_list = list(self.active_dms.items())
74:         if dm_idx < len(dm_list):
75:             nick, peer_id = dm_list[dm_idx]
76:             self.current_mode = PrivateDM(nick, peer_id)
77:             print("\033[90m─────────────────────────\033[0m")
78:             print(f"\033[90m» Switched to DM with {nick}. Just type to send messages.\033[0m")
79:             return True
80:         
81:         return False
82:     
83:     def add_channel(self, channel: str):
84:         if channel not in self.active_channels:
85:             self.active_channels.append(channel)
86:     
87:     def add_dm(self, nickname: str, peer_id: str):
88:         self.active_dms[nickname] = peer_id
89:     
90:     def enter_dm_mode(self, nickname: str, peer_id: str):
91:         self.add_dm(nickname, peer_id)
92:         self.current_mode = PrivateDM(nickname, peer_id)
93:         print("\033[90m─────────────────────────\033[0m")
94:         print(f"\033[90m» Entered DM mode with {nickname}. Just type to send messages.\033[0m")
95:     
96:     def switch_to_channel(self, channel: str):
97:         self.add_channel(channel)
98:         self.current_mode = Channel(channel)
99:         print("\033[90m─────────────────────────\033[0m")
100:         print(f"\033[90m» Switched to channel {channel}\033[0m")
101:     
102:     def switch_to_channel_silent(self, channel: str):
103:         self.add_channel(channel)
104:         self.current_mode = Channel(channel)
105:     
106:     def switch_to_public(self):
107:         self.current_mode = Public()
108:         print("\033[90m─────────────────────────\033[0m")
109:         print("\033[90m» Switched to Public chat. Just type to send messages.\033[0m")
110:     
111:     def remove_channel(self, channel: str):
112:         if channel in self.active_channels:
113:             self.active_channels.remove(channel)
114:     
115:     def show_conversation_list(self):
116:         print("\n╭─── Active Conversations ───╮")
117:         print("│                            │")
118:         
119:         # Public
120:         indicator = "→" if isinstance(self.current_mode, Public) else " "
121:         print(f"│ {indicator} [1] Public              │")
122:

127:             indicator = "→" if is_current else " "

130:             num += 1

135:             indicator = "→" if is_current else " "
136:             dm_text = f"DM: {nick}"
137:             padding = " " * (18 - len(dm_text))
138:             print(f"│ {indicator} [{num}] {dm_text}{padding}│")
139:             num += 1
140:         
141:         print("│                            │")
142:         print("╰────────────────────────────╯")
143:     
144:     def get_conversation_list_with_numbers(self) -> str:
145:         output = "╭─── Select Conversation ───╮\n"
146:

149:

154:             output += f"│  {num}. {channel}{padding}│\n"
155:             num += 1

167: def format_message_display(
168:     timestamp: datetime,
169:     sender: str,
170:     content: str,
171:     is_private: bool,
172:     is_channel: bool,
173:     channel_name: Optional[str],
174:     recipient: Optional[str],
175:     my_nickname: str
176: ) -> str:
177:     """Format a message for display"""
178:     time_str = timestamp.strftime("%H:%M")
179:     
180:     if is_private:
181:         # Orange for private messages (matching iOS)
182:         if sender == my_nickname:
183:             # Message I sent - use brighter orange
184:             if recipient:
185:                 return f"\033[2;38;5;208m[{time_str}|DM]\033[0m \033[38;5;214m<you → {recipient}>\033[0m {content}"
186:             else:
187:                 return f"\033[2;38;5;208m[{time_str}|DM]\033[0m \033[38;5;214m<you → ???>\033[0m {content}"
188:         else:
189:             # Message I received - use normal orange
190:             return f"\033[2;38;5;208m[{time_str}|DM]\033[0m \033[38;5;208m<{sender} → you>\033[0m {content}"
191:     elif is_channel:
192:         # Blue for channel messages (matching iOS)
193:         if sender == my_nickname:
194:             # My messages - light blue (256-color)
195:             if channel_name:
196:                 return f"\033[2;34m[{time_str}|{channel_name}]\033[0m \033[38;5;117m<{sender} @ {channel_name}>\033[0m {content}"
197:             else:
198:                 return f"\033[2;34m[{time_str}|Ch]\033[0m \033[38;5;117m<{sender} @ ???>\033[0m {content}"
199:         else:
200:             # Other users - normal blue
201:             if channel_name:
202:                 return f"\033[2;34m[{time_str}|{channel_name}]\033[0m \033[34m<{sender} @ {channel_name}>\033[0m {content}"
203:             else:
204:                 return f"\033[2;34m[{time_str}|Ch]\033[0m \033[34m<{sender} @ ???>\033[0m {content}"
205:     else:
206:         # Public message - green for metadata

from datetime import datetime
from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Tuple
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class ChatMode:
    """Base class for chat modes"""
    pass

@dataclass
class Public(ChatMode):
    """Public chat mode"""
    pass

@dataclass
class Channel(ChatMode):
    """Channel chat mode"""
    name: str

@dataclass
class PrivateDM(ChatMode):
    """Private DM mode"""
    nickname: str
    peer_id: str

class ChatContext:
    def __init__(self):
        self.current_mode: ChatMode = Public()
        self.active_channels: List[str] = []
        self.active_dms: Dict[str, str] = {}  # nickname -> peer_id
        self.last_private_sender: Optional[Tuple[str, str]] = None
    
    def format_prompt(self) -> str:
        if isinstance(self.current_mode, Public):
            return "[Public]"
        elif isinstance(self.current_mode, Channel):
            return f"[{self.current_mode.name}]"
        elif isinstance(self.current_mode, PrivateDM):
            return f"[DM: {self.current_mode.nickname}]"
        return ">"
    
    def get_status_line(self) -> str:
        parts = ["[1] Public"]
        
        for i, channel in enumerate(self.active_channels):
            parts.append(f"[{i + 2}] {channel}")
        
        dm_start = 2 + len(self.active_channels)
        for i, (nick, _) in enumerate(self.active_dms.items()):
            parts.append(f"[{i + dm_start}] DM:{nick}")
        
        return f"Active: {' '.join(parts)}"
    
    def switch_to_number(self, num: int) -> bool:
        if num == 1:
            self.current_mode = Public()
            print("\033[90m─────────────────────────\033[0m")
            print("\033[90m» Switched to Public chat. Just type to send messages.\033[0m")
            return True
        
        channel_end = 1 + len(self.active_channels)
        if 1 < num <= channel_end:
            channel_idx = num - 2
            if channel_idx < len(self.active_channels):
                channel = self.active_channels[channel_idx]
                self.current_mode = Channel(channel)
                print("\033[90m─────────────────────────\033[0m")
                print(f"\033[90m» Switched to channel {channel}\033[0m")
                return True
        
        dm_start = channel_end + 1
        dm_idx = num - dm_start
        dm_list = list(self.active_dms.items())
        if dm_idx < len(dm_list):
            nick, peer_id = dm_list[dm_idx]
            self.current_mode = PrivateDM(nick, peer_id)
            print("\033[90m─────────────────────────\033[0m")
            print(f"\033[90m» Switched to DM with {nick}. Just type to send messages.\033[0m")
            return True
        
        return False
    
    def add_channel(self, channel: str):
        if channel not in self.active_channels:
            self.active_channels.append(channel)
    
    def add_dm(self, nickname: str, peer_id: str):
        self.active_dms[nickname] = peer_id
    
    def enter_dm_mode(self, nickname: str, peer_id: str):
        self.add_dm(nickname, peer_id)
        self.current_mode = PrivateDM(nickname, peer_id)
        print("\033[90m─────────────────────────\033[0m")
        print(f"\033[90m» Entered DM mode with {nickname}. Just type to send messages.\033[0m")
    
    def switch_to_channel(self, channel: str):
        self.add_channel(channel)
        self.current_mode = Channel(channel)
        print("\033[90m─────────────────────────\033[0m")
        print(f"\033[90m» Switched to channel {channel}\033[0m")
    
    def switch_to_channel_silent(self, channel: str):
        self.add_channel(channel)
        self.current_mode = Channel(channel)
    
    def switch_to_public(self):
        self.current_mode = Public()
        print("\033[90m─────────────────────────\033[0m")
        print("\033[90m» Switched to Public chat. Just type to send messages.\033[0m")
    
    def remove_channel(self, channel: str):
        if channel in self.active_channels:
            self.active_channels.remove(channel)
    
    def show_conversation_list(self):
        print("\n╭─── Active Conversations ───╮")
        print("│                            │")
        
        # Public
        indicator = "→" if isinstance(self.current_mode, Public) else " "
        print(f"│ {indicator} [1] Public              │")
        
        # Channels
        num = 2
        for channel in self.active_channels:
            is_current = isinstance(self.current_mode, Channel) and self.current_mode.name == channel
            indicator = "→" if is_current else " "
            padding = " " * (18 - len(channel))
            print(f"│ {indicator} [{num}] {channel}{padding}│")
            num += 1
        
        # DMs
        for nick, _ in self.active_dms.items():
            is_current = isinstance(self.current_mode, PrivateDM) and self.current_mode.nickname == nick
            indicator = "→" if is_current else " "
            dm_text = f"DM: {nick}"
            padding = " " * (18 - len(dm_text))
            print(f"│ {indicator} [{num}] {dm_text}{padding}│")
            num += 1
        
        print("│                            │")
        print("╰────────────────────────────╯")
    
    def get_conversation_list_with_numbers(self) -> str:
        output = "╭─── Select Conversation ───╮\n"
        
        # Public
        output += "│  1. Public                │\n"
        
        # Channels
        num = 2
        for channel in self.active_channels:
            padding = " " * (20 - len(channel))
            output += f"│  {num}. {channel}{padding}│\n"
            num += 1
        
        # DMs
        for nick, _ in self.active_dms.items():
            dm_text = f"DM: {nick}"
            padding = " " * (20 - len(dm_text))
            output += f"│  {num}. {dm_text}{padding}│\n"
            num += 1
        
        output += "╰───────────────────────────╯"
        return output

def format_message_display(
    timestamp: datetime,
    sender: str,
    content: str,
    is_private: bool,
    is_channel: bool,
    channel_name: Optional[str],
    recipient: Optional[str],
    my_nickname: str
) -> str:
    """Format a message for display"""
    time_str = timestamp.strftime("%H:%M")
    
    if is_private:
        # Orange for private messages (matching iOS)
        if sender == my_nickname:
            # Message I sent - use brighter orange
            if recipient:
                return f"\033[2;38;5;208m[{time_str}|DM]\033[0m \033[38;5;214m<you → {recipient}>\033[0m {content}"
            else:
                return f"\033[2;38;5;208m[{time_str}|DM]\033[0m \033[38;5;214m<you → ???>\033[0m {content}"
        else:
            # Message I received - use normal orange
            return f"\033[2;38;5;208m[{time_str}|DM]\033[0m \033[38;5;208m<{sender} → you>\033[0m {content}"
    elif is_channel:
        # Blue for channel messages (matching iOS)
        if sender == my_nickname:
            # My messages - light blue (256-color)
            if channel_name:
                return f"\033[2;34m[{time_str}|{channel_name}]\033[0m \033[38;5;117m<{sender} @ {channel_name}>\033[0m {content}"
            else:
                return f"\033[2;34m[{time_str}|Ch]\033[0m \033[38;5;117m<{sender} @ ???>\033[0m {content}"
        else:
            # Other users - normal blue
            if channel_name:
                return f"\033[2;34m[{time_str}|{channel_name}]\033[0m \033[34m<{sender} @ {channel_name}>\033[0m {content}"
            else:
                return f"\033[2;34m[{time_str}|Ch]\033[0m \033[34m<{sender} @ ???>\033[0m {content}"
    else:
        # Public message - green for metadata
        if sender == my_nickname:
            # My messages - light green (256-color)
            return f"\033[2;32m[{time_str}]\033[0m \033[38;5;120m<{sender}>\033[0m {content}"
        else:
            # Other users - normal green
            return f"\033[2;32m[{time_str}]\033[0m \033[32m<{sender}>\033[0m {content}"

def print_help():
    """Print help menu"""
    print("\n\033[38;5;46m━━━ BitChat Commands ━━━\033[0m\n")
    
    # General
    print("\033[38;5;40m▶ General\033[0m")
    print("  \033[36m/help\033[0m         Show this help menu")
    print("  \033[36m/name\033[0m \033[90m<name>\033[0m  Change your nickname")
    print("  \033[36m/status\033[0m       Show connection info")
    print("  \033[36m/clear\033[0m        Clear the screen")
    print("  \033[36m/exit\033[0m         Quit BitChat\n")
    
    # Navigation
    print("\033[38;5;40m▶ Navigation\033[0m")
    print("  \033[36m1-9\033[0m           Quick switch to conversation")
    print("  \033[36m/list\033[0m         Show all conversations")
    print("  \033[36m/switch\033[0m       Interactive conversation switcher")
    print("  \033[36m/public\033[0m       Go to public chat\n")
    
    # Messaging
    print("\033[38;5;40m▶ Messaging\033[0m")
    print("  \033[90m(type normally to send in current mode)\033[0m")
    print("  \033[36m/dm\033[0m \033[90m<name>\033[0m    Start private conversation")
    print("  \033[36m/dm\033[0m \033[90m<name> <msg>\033[0m Send quick private message")
    print("  \033[36m/reply\033[0m        Reply to last private message\n")
    
    # Channels
    print("\033[38;5;40m▶ Channels\033[0m")
    print("  \033[36m/j\033[0m \033[90m#channel\033[0m   Join or create a channel")
    print("  \033[36m/j\033[0m \033[90m#channel <password>\033[0m Join with password")
    print("  \033[36m/leave\033[0m        Leave current channel")
    print("  \033[36m/pass\033[0m \033[90m<pwd>\033[0m   Set channel password (owner only)")
    print("  \033[36m/transfer\033[0m \033[90m@user\033[0m Transfer ownership (owner only)\n")
    
    # Discovery
    print("\033[38;5;40m▶ Discovery\033[0m")
    print("  \033[36m/channels\033[0m     List all discovered channels")
    print("  \033[36m/online\033[0m       Show who's online")
    print("  \033[36m/w\033[0m            Alias for /online\n")
    
    # Privacy & Security
    print("\033[38;5;40m▶ Privacy & Security\033[0m")
    print("  \033[36m/block\033[0m \033[90m@user\033[0m  Block a user")
    print("  \033[36m/block\033[0m        List blocked users")
    print("  \033[36m/unblock\033[0m \033[90m@user\033[0m Unblock a user\n")
    
    print("\033[38;5;40m━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\033[0m")

def clear_screen():
    """Clear the terminal screen"""
    print("\033[2J\033[1;1H", end='')

# Export classes
__all__ = ['ChatMode', 'Public', 'Channel', 'PrivateDM', 'ChatContext', 'format_message_display', 'print_help', 'clear_screen']

Now I understand the current state of the repo. It's a Python-based BitChat client that uses BLE (Bluetooth Low Energy) for peer-to-peer communication. The user wants me to use this as a basis for a Python-based "bitchat node daemon" - meaning they want to convert this from a BLE-based interactive client into a network-based daemon/service that runs in the background.

Let me explore the remaining files to understand the full codebase.

1231:                 debug_println(f"[NOISE] We should initiate handshake with {peer_id}")
1232:                 # Check if we already have a session or ongoing handshake
1233:                 if not self.encryption_service.is_session_established(peer_id):
1234:                     try:
1235:                         handshake_message = self.encryption_service.initiate_handshake(peer_id)
1236:                         handshake_packet = create_bitchat_packet_with_recipient(
1237:                             self.my_peer_id, peer_id, MessageType.NOISE_HANDSHAKE_INIT, handshake_message, None
1238:                         )
1239:                         # Set TTL to 3 like iOS
1240:                         handshake_data = bytearray(handshake_packet)
1241:                         handshake_data[2] = 3
1242:                         handshake_packet = bytes(handshake_data)
1243:                         await self.send_packet(handshake_packet)
1244:                         debug_println(f"[NOISE] Initiated handshake with {peer_id}")
1245:                     except Exception as e:
1246:                         debug_println(f"[NOISE] Failed to initiate handshake: {e}")
1247:             else:
1248:                 debug_println(f"[NOISE] Waiting for {peer_id} to initiate handshake")
1249:                     
1250:         except Exception as e:
1251:             debug_println(f"[NOISE] Error handling identity announcement: {e}")
1252:             import traceback
1253:             debug_println(f"[NOISE] Identity announce error details: {traceback.format_exc()}")
1254:     
1255:     def parse_noise_identity_announcement_binary(self, data: bytes) -> dict:
1256:         """Parse binary format noise identity announcement matching iOS appendData format"""
1257:         try:
1258:             offset = 0
1259:             
1260:             debug_println(f"[NOISE] Parsing binary announcement, total length: {len(data)}")
1261:             debug_println(f"[NOISE] Raw data (hex): {data.hex()}")
1262:             
1263:             # Read flags byte
1264:             if offset >= len(data):
1265:                 debug_println("[NOISE] Error: Not enough data for flags")
1266:                 return None
1267:             flags = data[offset]
1268:             offset += 1
1269:             debug_println(f"[NOISE] Flags: 0x{flags:02x}")
1270:             
1271:             # Check if previousPeerID is present (flag bit 0)
1272:             has_previous_peer_id = (flags & 0x01) != 0
1273:             debug_println(f"[NOISE] Has previous peer ID: {has_previous_peer_id}")
1274:             
1275:             # Read peerID (8 bytes)
1276:             if offset + 8 > len(data):
1277:                 debug_println(f"[NOISE] Error: Not enough data for peerID, need 8 bytes, have {len(data) - offset}")
1278:                 return None
1279:             peer_id = data[offset:offset+8].hex()
1280:             offset += 8
1281:             debug_println(f"[NOISE] Peer ID: {peer_id}")
1282:             
1283:             # Read publicKey using appendData format (1-byte length prefix for 255 max)
1284:             if offset >= len(data):
1285:                 debug_println("[NOISE] Error: Not enough data for publicKey length")
1286:                 return None
1287:             pub_key_len = data[offset]
1288:             offset += 1
1289:             
1290:             if offset + pub_key_len > len(data):
1291:                 debug_println(f"[NOISE] Error: Not enough data for publicKey, need {pub_key_len} bytes, have {len(data) - offset}")
1292:                 return None
1293:             public_key = data[offset:offset+pub_key_len]
1294:             offset += pub_key_len
1295:             debug_println(f"[NOISE] Public key length: {pub_key_len}, key: {public_key.hex()}")
1296:             
1297:             # Read signingPublicKey using appendData format (1-byte length prefix for 255 max)
1298:             if offset >= len(data):
1299:                 debug_println("[NOISE] Error: Not enough data for signingPublicKey length")
1300:                 return None
1301:             signing_key_len = data[offset]
1302:             offset += 1
1303:             
1304:             if offset + signing_key_len > len(data):
1305:                 debug_println(f"[NOISE] Error: Not enough data for signingPublicKey, need {signing_key_len} bytes, have {len(data) - offset}")
1306:                 return None
1307:             signing_public_key = data[offset:offset+signing_key_len]
1308:             offset += signing_key_len
1309:             debug_println(f"[NOISE] Signing public key length: {signing_key_len}, key: {signing_public_key.hex()}")
1310:             
1311:             # Read nickname using appendString format (1-byte length prefix for 255 max)
1312:             if offset >= len(data):
1313:                 debug_println("[NOISE] Error: Not enough data for nickname length")
1314:                 return None
1315:             nickname_len = data[offset]
1316:             offset += 1
1317:             debug_println(f"[NOISE] Nickname length: {nickname_len}")
1318:             
1319:             nickname = ""
1320:             if nickname_len > 0:
1321:                 if offset + nickname_len > len(data):
1322:                     debug_println(f"[NOISE] Error: Not enough data for nickname, need {nickname_len} bytes, have {len(data) - offset}")
1323:                     return None
1324:                 nickname_bytes = data[offset:offset+nickname_len]
1325:                 offset += nickname_len
1326:                 nickname = nickname_bytes.decode('utf-8')
1327:                 debug_println(f"[NOISE] Nickname: '{nickname}'")
1328:             else:
1329:                 debug_println("[NOISE] Nickname: (empty)")
1330:             
1331:             # Read timestamp using appendDate format (8-byte UInt64 in milliseconds, big-endian)
1332:             if offset + 8 > len(data):
1333:                 debug_println(f"[NOISE] Error: Not enough data for timestamp, need 8 bytes, have {len(data) - offset}")
1334:                 return None
1335:             timestamp_ms = int.from_bytes(data[offset:offset+8], byteorder='big')
1336:             offset += 8
1337:             timestamp = timestamp_ms / 1000.0  # Convert from milliseconds to seconds
1338:             debug_println(f"[NOISE] Timestamp: {timestamp} ({timestamp_ms}ms)")
1339:             
1340:             # Read previousPeerID if present (8 bytes)
1341:             previous_peer_id = None
1342:             if has_previous_peer_id:
1343:                 if offset + 8 > len(data):
1344:                     debug_println("[NOISE] Error: Not enough data for previousPeerID")
1345:                     return None
1346:                 previous_peer_id = data[offset:offset+8].hex()
1347:                 offset += 8
1348:                 debug_println(f"[NOISE] Previous peer ID: {previous_peer_id}")
1349:             
1350:             # Read signature using appendData format (1-byte length prefix for 255 max)
1351:             if offset >= len(data):
1352:                 debug_println("[NOISE] Error: Not enough data for signature length")
1353:                 return None
1354:             signature_len = data[offset]
1355:             offset += 1
1356:             
1357:             if offset + signature_len > len(data):
1358:                 debug_println(f"[NOISE] Error: Not enough data for signature, need {signature_len} bytes, have {len(data) - offset}")
1359:                 return None
1360:             signature = data[offset:offset+signature_len]
1361:             offset += signature_len
1362:             debug_println(f"[NOISE] Signature length: {signature_len}, sig: {signature.hex()}")
1363:             
1364:             debug_println(f"[NOISE] Total parsed {offset} bytes out of {len(data)} available")
1365:             
1366:             return {
1367:                 'peerID': peer_id,
1368:                 'publicKey': public_key.hex(),
1369:                 'signingPublicKey': signing_public_key.hex(),
1370:                 'nickname': nickname,
1371:                 'timestamp': timestamp,
1372:                 'signature': signature.hex(),
1373:                 'previousPeerID': previous_peer_id,
1374:                 'truncated': False
1375:             }
1376:             
1377:         except Exception as e:
1378:             debug_println(f"[NOISE] Error parsing binary announcement: {e}")
1379:             import traceback
1380:             debug_println(f"[NOISE] Binary parser error details: {traceback.format_exc()}")
1381:             return None
1382:     
1383:     def encode_noise_identity_announcement_binary(self, peer_id: str, public_key: bytes, 
1384:                                                   signing_public_key: bytes, nickname: str, 
1385:                                                   timestamp: int, signature: bytes, 
1386:                                                   previous_peer_id: str = None) -> bytes:
1387:         """Encode noise identity announcement to binary format matching iOS appendData format"""
1388:         data = bytearray()
1389:         
1390:         # Flags byte: bit 0 = hasPreviousPeerID
1391:         flags = 0
1392:         if previous_peer_id:
1393:             flags |= 0x01
1394:         data.append(flags)
1395:         
1396:         # PeerID as 8-byte hex string (match Swift conversion)
1397:         peer_data = bytes.fromhex(peer_id.ljust(16, '0')[:16])  # Pad to 8 bytes
1398:         data.extend(peer_data)
1399:         
1400:         # PublicKey using appendData format (1-byte length prefix since 32 < 255)
1401:         data.append(len(public_key))
1402:         data.extend(public_key)
1403:         
1404:         # SigningPublicKey using appendData format (1-byte length prefix since 32 < 255)
1405:         data.append(len(signing_public_key))
1406:         data.extend(signing_public_key)
1407:         
1408:         # Nickname using appendString format (1-byte length prefix for strings under 255 chars)
1409:         nickname_bytes = nickname.encode('utf-8')
1410:         data.append(len(nickname_bytes))
1411:         data.extend(nickname_bytes)
1412:         
1413:         # Timestamp using appendDate format (8 bytes UInt64 milliseconds, big-endian)
1414:         timestamp_ms = int(timestamp * 1000)  # Convert to milliseconds
1415:         for i in range(8):
1416:             data.append((timestamp_ms >> ((7-i) * 8)) & 0xFF)
1417:         
1418:         # PreviousPeerID if present (8 bytes, after timestamp)
1419:         if previous_peer_id:
1420:             prev_data = bytes.fromhex(previous_peer_id.ljust(16, '0')[:16])  # Pad to 8 bytes
1421:             data.extend(prev_data)
1422:         
1423:         # Signature using appendData format (1-byte length prefix)
1424:         data.append(len(signature))
1425:         data.extend(signature)
1426:         
1427:         return bytes(data)
1428:     
1429:     async def send_delivery_ack(self, message_id: str, sender_id: str, is_private: bool):
1430:         """Send delivery acknowledgment"""
1431:         ack_id = f"{message_id}-{self.my_peer_id}"
1432:         if not self.delivery_tracker.should_send_ack(ack_id):
1433:             return
1434:         
1435:         debug_println(f"[ACK] Sending delivery ACK for message {message_id}")
1436:         
1437:         ack = DeliveryAck(
1438:             message_id,
1439:             str(uuid.uuid4()),
1440:             self.my_peer_id,
1441:             self.nickname,
1442:             int(time.time() * 1000),
1443:             1
1444:         )
1445:         
1446:         ack_payload = json.dumps({
1447:             'originalMessageID': ack.original_message_id,
1448:             'ackID': ack.ack_id,
1449:             'recipientID': ack.recipient_id,
1450:             'recipientNickname': ack.recipient_nickname,
1451:             'timestamp': ack.timestamp,
1452:             'hopCount': ack.hop_count
1453:         }).encode()
1454:         
1455:         # Encrypt if private
1456:         if is_private:
1457:             try:
1458:                 ack_payload = self.encryption_service.encrypt(ack_payload, sender_id)
1459:             except:
1460:                 pass
1461:         
1462:         # Send ACK packet
1463:         ack_packet = create_bitchat_packet_with_recipient(
1464:             self.my_peer_id,
1465:             sender_id,
1466:             MessageType.DELIVERY_ACK,
1467:             ack_payload,
1468:             None
1469:         )
1470:         
1471:         # Set TTL to 3
1472:         ack_packet_data = bytearray(ack_packet)
1473:         ack_packet_data[2] = 3
1474:         
1475:         await self.send_packet(bytes(ack_packet_data))

1477:     async def send_channel_announce(self, channel: str, is_protected: bool, key_commitment: Optional[str]):
1478:         """Send channel announcement"""
1479:         payload = f"{channel}|{'1' if is_protected else '0'}|{self.my_peer_id}|{key_commitment or ''}"
1480:         packet = create_bitchat_packet(
1481:             self.my_peer_id,
1482:             MessageType.CHANNEL_ANNOUNCE,
1483:             payload.encode()
1484:         )
1485:         
1486:         # Set TTL to 5
1487:         packet_data = bytearray(packet)
1488:         packet_data[2] = 5
1489:         
1490:         debug_println(f"[CHANNEL] Sending channel announce for {channel}")
1491:         await self.send_packet(bytes(packet_data))

1507:     async def handle_user_input(self, line: str):
1508:         """Handle user input commands and messages"""
1509:         # Number switching
1510:         if len(line) == 1 and line.isdigit():
1511:             num = int(line)
1512:             if self.chat_context.switch_to_number(num):
1513:                 debug_println(self.chat_context.get_status_line())
1514:             else:
1515:                 print("» Invalid conversation number")
1516:             return
1517:         
1518:         # Commands

1520:             print_help()
1521:             return
1522:         
1523:         if line == "/exit":
1524:             # Send leave notification if connected
1525:             if self.client and self.client.is_connected:
1526:                 leave_packet = create_bitchat_packet(
1527:                     self.my_peer_id, MessageType.LEAVE, self.nickname.encode()
1528:                 )
1529:                 await self.send_packet(leave_packet)
1530:                 await asyncio.sleep(0.1)  # Give time for the packet to send
1531:             
1532:             await self.save_app_state()
1533:             self.running = False
1534:             return
1535:         
1536:         if line.startswith("/name "):
1537:             new_name = line[6:].strip()
1538:             if not new_name:
1539:                 print("\033[93m⚠ Usage: /name <new_nickname>\033[0m")
1540:                 print("\033[90mExample: /name Alice\033[0m")
1541:             elif len(new_name) > 20:
1542:                 print("\033[93m⚠ Nickname too long\033[0m")
1543:                 print("\033[90mMaximum 20 characters allowed.\033[0m")
1544:             elif not all(c.isalnum() or c in '-_' for c in new_name):
1545:                 print("\033[93m⚠ Invalid nickname\033[0m")
1546:                 print("\033[90mNicknames can only contain letters, numbers, hyphens and underscores.\033[0m")
1547:             elif new_name in ["system", "all"]:
1548:                 print("\033[93m⚠ Reserved nickname\033[0m")
1549:                 print("\033[90mThis nickname is reserved and cannot be used.\033[0m")
1550:             else:
1551:                 self.nickname = new_name
1552:                 announce_packet = create_bitchat_packet(
1553:                     self.my_peer_id, MessageType.ANNOUNCE, self.nickname.encode()
1554:                 )
1555:                 await self.send_packet(announce_packet)
1556:                 print(f"\033[90m» Nickname changed to: {self.nickname}\033[0m")
1557:                 await self.save_app_state()
1558:             return
1559:         
1560:         if line == "/list":
1561:             self.chat_context.show_conversation_list()
1562:             return
1563:         
1564:         if line == "/switch":
1565:             print(f"\n{self.chat_context.get_conversation_list_with_numbers()}")
1566:             switch_input = await aioconsole.ainput("Enter number to switch to: ")
1567:             if switch_input.strip().isdigit():
1568:                 num = int(switch_input.strip())
1569:                 if self.chat_context.switch_to_number(num):
1570:                     debug_println(self.chat_context.get_status_line())
1571:                 else:
1572:                     print("» Invalid selection")
1573:             return
1574:         
1575:         if line.startswith("/j "):
1576:             await self.handle_join_channel(line)
1577:             return
1578:         
1579:         if line == "/public":
1580:             self.chat_context.switch_to_public()
1581:             debug_println(self.chat_context.get_status_line())
1582:             return
1583:         
1584:         if line in ["/online", "/w"]:
1585:             if not self.client or not self.client.is_connected:
1586:                 print("» You're not connected to any peers yet.")
1587:                 print("\033[90mWaiting for other BitChat devices...\033[0m")
1588:             else:

1593:                     print("» No one else is online right now.")

1733:         """Handle /j command"""
1734:         parts = line.split()
1735:         if len(parts) < 2:
1736:             print("\033[93m⚠ Usage: /j #<channel> [password]\033[0m")
